A_intro: supervisors and teacher trainers in March 1952 and a committee was
A_methods:
appointed to plan a definite agenda for student teachers at the F.F.A.
Convention in October. Members of the committee were Howard Bradley,
Kansas State College; Raymond Clark, Michigan State College; Cc. V.
Roderick, University of Missouri; Roy Dillon, Teacher, Malden, Illinois;
J. N. Weiss, University of Illinois. It was also requested that a copy
of the agenda be sent to Head Teacher Trainers and Supervisors of Student
Teaching in the Region. Action taken at the Kansas City conference in
1953 included a resolution for "holding the Student Teacher Conference
A_results:
on a National Scale." .
It was quite logical that special conferences for prospective
teachers of vocational agriculture be held in connection with the National
F.F.A. Convention. By manipulation of schedules the trainees have acceas
to special convention features including the National F.F.A. Speaking
Contest and the awarding of American Farmer Degrees. A substantial —
number of the student teachers are members of the Alpha Tau Alpha fraternity which holds an annual conclave during the F.F.A. Convention week.
Typically, agenda items for Student Teachers Conference includes
a reception and coffee hour sponsored by the National Vocational Agriculture Teachers Association, opportunities to meet National F.F.A.
officers and award winners, talks by experienced and beginning teachers,
and round-table discussions of student teaching experiences.
Approximately two-hundred persons have registered annually for
the National Student Teachers Conference since the activity was organized
in 1952 in connection with the National F.F.A. Convention. .
